data.title: Whale Opens Significant Long Position on Bitcoin
data.description: According to PANews, a whale identified by Lookonchain monitoring has recently opened a substantial long position on Bitcoin. Four hours ago, the entity, known as 0x0ddf, purchased 1,000 BTC, valued at approximately $95.3 million. The liquidation price for this position is set at $60,042.

data.title: U.S. Labor Department to Release September Employment Report
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced on Friday that it will release the highly anticipated September employment report next Thursday. The report was initially scheduled for release on October 3 but was delayed due to a 43-day government shutdown.

data.title: Canary Capital Pauses New Crypto ETF Applications Amid Regulatory Considerations
data.description: According to Foresight News, CoinDesk reports that Canary Capital CEO Steve McClurg announced the company has submitted all eligible ETF applications under current regulatory guidelines. For the remainder of the year, the company may halt new crypto ETF applications. The decision to file additional documents will depend on regulatory changes or whether new assets meet the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) requirements. Canary Capital will focus on managing its newly launched XRP ETF, which recorded a first-day trading volume of $58 million.

data.title: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has added "HKTWeb3" to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms.
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has added a virtual asset trading platform called "HKTWeb3" to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. The SFC stated that the platform is not licensed by the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission and its website claims to cooperate with a virtual asset trading platform licensed by the SFC, but the platform has confirmed that it has no relationship with HKTWeb3.

data.title: The crypto fear and greed index fell to 10, its lowest level since February 27.
data.description: Alternative data shows that the cryptocurrency fear and greed index is 10 today (up from 16 yesterday), the lowest level since February 27th. The average for last week was 20, indicating the market remains in a state of "extreme fear." According to Coingecko data, the current total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies has fallen to approximately $33,000, a 24-hour drop of 2.8%.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index fell to 10, the lowest level since February 27; 2. Over $1 billion in liquidations occurred across the crypto network in the past 24 hours, mainly long positions; 3. The September non-farm payroll report will be released next Thursday; 4. Ethereum treasury company BitMine appointed a new CEO and expanded its board of directors; 5. Tether: has deployed $1.5 billion to date to expand commodity trade finance and explore a new model of stablecoin + physical assets; 6. Canary Capital may suspend new ETF applications after launching XRP; 7. Multicoin Capital Liquid Fund invested in Ethena Protocol's native token ENA; 8. Yala stablecoin YU exhibited suspicious activity similar to USDX, with related addresses lending USDC at extremely high interest rates but failing to repay; 9. Federal Reserve's Bostic: has not yet decided whether to approve a rate cut next month.

data.description:
As regulatory clarity arrives, Visa sees its role evolving from payments network to essential infrastructure connecting traditional finance with blockchain-based money movement

data.title: Tether Expands into Commodity Trade Financing with $1.5 Billion Credit Deployment
data.description: According to Odaily, Tether Holdings SA has announced the deployment of approximately $1.5 billion in credit lines within the commodity trade financing sector, with plans for significant expansion in this area. The company intends to leverage its asset reserves, which are nearly $200 billion, to provide loans in both U.S. dollars and its stablecoin, USDT. These loans will support transactions involving commodities such as oil, cotton, and wheat. This move is seen as a strategic shift for Tether from being solely a stablecoin issuer to becoming a comprehensive platform for digital assets and trade financing, potentially enhancing the integration of stablecoins with traditional physical assets.

data.title: Tether: To date, it has invested $1.5 billion to expand commodity trade financing and explore a new model of stablecoins + physical assets.
data.description: Tether Holdings SA announced it has deployed approximately $1.5 billion in credit facilities to the commodity trade finance sector and plans to "significantly expand" this business. The company stated it will leverage its nearly $200 billion in asset reserves to provide loans in the form of US dollars and its stablecoin USDT, covering commodity trading such as oil, cotton, and wheat. This move is seen as a strategic shift for Tether from a single stablecoin issuer to a comprehensive "digital asset + trade finance" platform, potentially driving further integration of stablecoins with traditional physical assets. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: After making nearly $3 million in profit from swing trading of ETH, a major whale then repurchased nearly 20,000 ETH.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale/institution that had profited $96.67 million through ETH trading sold 10,000 ETH at $3,597 on November 10, making a profit of $2.93 million. In the past day, it repurchased 19,945 ETH ($63.82 million) at an average price of $3,200 through Coinbase, Wintertermute, and FalconX.

data.title: Harvard University Increases Holdings in IBIT and GLD
data.description: According to PANews, as of September 30, Harvard University significantly increased its holdings in IBIT and GLD, as revealed by 13F filings. The university holds 6,813,612 shares of IBIT, valued at $442.9 million, and 661,391 shares of the GLD gold ETF, worth $235 million. This marks a substantial increase from the end of June, when Harvard held 1,906,000 shares of IBIT and 333,000 shares of GLD, reflecting growth rates of 257.48% and 98.62%, respectively. Additionally, Harvard owns 583,931 shares of Nvidia, valued at $109 million.

data.title: Crypto oversight by CFTC over SEC is ‘directionally correct’ — Jeff Park
data.description:

data.title: Multicoin co-founder: Large holdings have been built on ENA this year.
data.description: Multicoin co-founder Kyle Samani posted on X, "We've built a big position in ENA this year." This follows previous reports that Multicoin Capital's Liquid Fund announced an investment in Ethena Protocol's native token, ENA.

data.title: Canary Capital may suspend new ETF applications after launching XRP.
data.description: Canary Capital CEO Steve McClurg stated that the company has submitted applications for all currently eligible tokens under existing regulatory guidelines. The company may pause new cryptocurrency ETF applications for the remainder of the year, with the need for further documentation depending on regulatory changes or whether new assets meet the requirements of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The company will shift its focus to managing products such as its newly launched XRP ETF, which saw $58 million in trading volume on its first day. (CoinDesk)

data.description:

data.title: Yala's stablecoin YU is experiencing suspicious activity similar to USDX, with related addresses lending USDC at extremely high interest rates but failing to repay.
data.description: DeFi community member YAM posted on X that they have observed warning signs for Yala's stablecoin YU. An address closely associated with Yala is borrowing the full amount of USDC and most of YU from the Yala Frontier market on Euler, and despite persistently high interest rates, has not made any repayments. The market's liquidity utilization has reached 100%, meaning lenders are unable to withdraw any liquidity. The Euler team has also set the lending limit for the Yala Frontier market to zero. Reportedly, the Yala team has not yet responded to inquiries from the Euler team or Discord community users. YAM added that YU is currently still pegged to the exchange rate on Solana, and its liquidity pool still has nearly one million USDC available for withdrawal at the pegged price. This post is primarily a risk warning, and it is still uncertain whether Yala is truly in trouble.
